metadata.name: RFK Assassination Case Reasoning
metadata.description: Ontology modeling the assassination of Robert F. Kennedy (1968) as a structured evidence reasoning case — demonstrating how stubborn facts and actual contradictions ground legal analysis beyond LLM correlation
metadata.source: Historical record, autopsy reports, trial transcripts, academic analysis (Arthur Sarazin — Who killed Robert F. Kennedy?, 2026)

classes.label: Person
classes.description: Individual involved in the RFK assassination case — victim, accused, witness, investigator, or analyst

classes.label: Physical Evidence
classes.description: Tangible objects or forensic artifacts collected from the crime scene or victims

classes.label: Testimonial Evidence
classes.description: Sworn witness statements and testimony given during investigation or trial

classes.label: Forensic Finding
classes.description: Scientific or medical conclusion derived from examination of physical evidence

classes.label: Documentary Evidence
classes.description: Official documents, reports, and records produced during investigation or trial

classes.label: Legal Proceeding
classes.description: Formal legal process — trial, hearing, or petition

classes.label: Legal Argument
classes.description: Structured line of reasoning advanced by prosecution, defense, or analysts

classes.label: Contradiction
classes.description: Identified actual conflict between two or more pieces of evidence or claims — not merely apparent but logically irreconcilable

classes.label: Claim
classes.description: Factual assertion about what happened, supported or contradicted by evidence

classes.label: Verdict
classes.description: Formal legal determination or sentence issued by a court

classes.label: Location
classes.description: Physical place relevant to the case events

classes.label: Event
classes.description: Specific occurrence in time relevant to the case

classes.label: Evidence Assessment
classes.description: Structured evaluation of evidence support, conflict, or reasoning quality — the output of a reasoning workflow applied to case facts

instances.label: Robert F. Kennedy
instances.description: US Senator and presidential candidate, assassinated June 5, 1968 at the Ambassador Hotel in Los Angeles

instances.label: Sirhan Sirhan
instances.description: Convicted assassin of RFK — fired from in front of Kennedy, yet autopsy shows shots came from behind

instances.label: Thomas Noguchi
instances.description: Los Angeles County Chief Medical Examiner who performed the autopsy on RFK

instances.label: Karl Uecker
instances.description: Ambassador Hotel maitre d'hotel who was guiding RFK through the pantry and tackled Sirhan — key witness to shooter position and distance

instances.label: Thane Eugene Cesar
instances.description: Ace Guard Service security guard positioned directly behind RFK at the moment of shooting — admitted drawing his gun

instances.label: Paul Schrade
instances.description: United Auto Workers official, shot and wounded in the pantry — later became prominent advocate for case reinvestigation

instances.label: DeWayne Wolfer
instances.description: LAPD criminalist and firearms examiner who conducted the official ballistics analysis

instances.label: William Harper
instances.description: Independent criminalist who challenged the official ballistics findings, concluding bullet evidence was inconsistent with a single weapon

instances.label: Lawrence Lessig
instances.description: Harvard law professor, author of 'Code and Other Laws of Cyberspace', tested ChatGPT on the RFK case and concluded it gave wishy-washy answers

instances.label: Grant Cooper
instances.description: Defense attorney for Sirhan Sirhan at the 1969 trial

instances.label: Vincent Bugliosi
instances.description: Prominent attorney and author who analyzed inconsistencies in the RFK assassination case

instances.label: Iver Johnson .22 caliber revolver
instances.description: Sirhan's weapon — an 8-round .22 caliber Iver Johnson Cadet revolver, serial number H-53725

properties.evidence_statement: Sirhan Sirhan possessed and fired an 8-round .22 caliber Iver Johnson Cadet revolver
properties.context_description: The weapon was recovered at the scene after Sirhan was tackled by Karl Uecker and others

instances.label: Bullet fragments from RFK
instances.description: Bullet and bullet fragments recovered from Robert F. Kennedy's body during autopsy

properties.evidence_statement: Multiple bullet fragments were recovered from RFK's body, entering from behind
properties.context_description: Recovered during autopsy by Thomas Noguchi on June 6, 1968

instances.label: Bullet fragments from wounded bystanders
instances.description: Bullets and fragments recovered from five other shooting victims in the pantry

properties.evidence_statement: Five other people were shot in the pantry alongside RFK
properties.context_description: Paul Schrade, Ira Goldstein, William Weisel, Elizabeth Evans, and Irwin Stroll were all wounded

instances.label: Door frame bullet holes
instances.description: Bullet holes found in the pantry door frames — their number raised questions about whether more than 8 shots were fired

properties.evidence_statement: Additional bullet holes were found in door frames of the Ambassador Hotel pantry
properties.context_description: The door frames were later destroyed by LAPD, eliminating the possibility of independent re-examination

instances.label: Powder burn patterns
instances.description: Gunshot residue patterns found on RFK's clothing and skin near entry wounds

properties.evidence_statement: Dense powder burn patterns on RFK's coat and skin indicate a muzzle-to-target distance of 1 to 3 inches
properties.context_description: Powder tattooing (stippling) is a reliable forensic indicator of firing distance

instances.label: Karl Uecker testimony
instances.description: Uecker stated under oath that Sirhan never got closer than 1.5 to 2 feet from RFK and was firing from the front

properties.evidence_statement: Sirhan was in front of RFK and never got closer than 1.5 to 2 feet before being grabbed
properties.context_description: Uecker was physically guiding RFK through the pantry and was the first to tackle Sirhan

instances.label: Thane Cesar testimony
instances.description: Security guard Cesar admitted he drew his .22 caliber gun but claimed he did not fire it

properties.evidence_statement: Cesar, positioned directly behind RFK, admitted drawing his weapon but denied firing
properties.context_description: Cesar was holding RFK's right elbow and was knocked down when the shooting started

instances.label: Witness consensus on Sirhan position
instances.description: Multiple witnesses consistently placed Sirhan in front of and facing RFK at the time of shooting

properties.evidence_statement: All eyewitnesses who testified placed Sirhan Sirhan in front of Robert F. Kennedy
properties.context_description: No witness placed Sirhan behind Kennedy; the consensus is unanimous on the facing direction

instances.label: Paul Schrade testimony
instances.description: Schrade, himself a victim, testified about the shooting and later spent decades advocating for reinvestigation

properties.evidence_statement: Schrade stated he was shot by a bullet that could not have come from Sirhan's position
properties.context_description: At Sirhan's 2016 parole hearing, Schrade said: 'Sirhan did not shoot Robert Kennedy'

instances.label: Autopsy: all shots from behind
instances.description: Thomas Noguchi determined that all three bullets that struck RFK entered from behind and to the right

properties.evidence_statement: All bullets entered RFK from behind and slightly to the right, at upward angles
properties.context_description: This is the most critical forensic finding — it directly contradicts Sirhan's known position in front of Kennedy

instances.label: Autopsy: muzzle distance 1-3 inches
instances.description: Powder burns on RFK's coat and skin indicated the fatal shots were fired from a distance of 1 to 3 inches

properties.evidence_statement: The muzzle of the gun that killed RFK was 1 to 3 inches from his body
properties.context_description: This is essentially contact range — inconsistent with witnesses who placed Sirhan 1.5 to 6 feet away

instances.label: Autopsy: fatal shot behind right ear
instances.description: The fatal bullet entered behind RFK's right ear and traveled forward into the brain

properties.evidence_statement: The fatal wound was a bullet entering behind the right ear, fired at near-contact range from behind
properties.context_description: This wound path is physically impossible from Sirhan's known position in front of Kennedy

instances.label: Autopsy: three bullet entry wounds
instances.description: RFK was struck by three separate bullets, all entering from behind

properties.evidence_statement: Three bullets hit RFK: one behind the right ear (fatal), one in the right armpit, one 1 inch from the armpit wound
properties.context_description: A fourth bullet passed through the shoulder pad of his suit coat without entering his body

instances.label: Bullet count analysis
instances.description: Analysis questioning whether more than 8 bullets were fired — Sirhan's revolver held only 8 rounds

properties.evidence_statement: Accounting for bullets in victims (7) plus door frame holes (at least 2) suggests more than 8 shots
properties.context_description: If the total exceeds 8, a second gun must have been present

instances.label: Wolfer ballistics report
instances.description: LAPD criminalist DeWayne Wolfer concluded all recovered bullets came from Sirhan's gun

properties.evidence_statement: Official LAPD ballistics report concluded all bullets matched Sirhan's Iver Johnson revolver
properties.context_description: Wolfer's methodology and conclusions were later challenged by independent experts

instances.label: Harper ballistics challenge
instances.description: Independent criminalist William Harper found that the bullet removed from RFK and the bullet removed from Weisel could not have been fired from the same gun

properties.evidence_statement: Bullet from RFK and bullet from victim Weisel show different rifling characteristics — two guns
properties.context_description: Harper filed an affidavit in 1970 challenging Wolfer's single-gun conclusion

instances.label: Powder burn analysis
instances.description: Scientific analysis of gunshot residue patterns confirming contact-to-near-contact firing range

properties.evidence_statement: Powder tattooing around entry wounds confirms firing distance of 1-3 inches maximum
properties.context_description: This finding is consistent across both Noguchi's autopsy and independent reviews

instances.label: Noguchi autopsy report
instances.description: Official autopsy report of Robert F. Kennedy by LA County Chief Medical Examiner Thomas Noguchi, dated June 6, 1968

properties.evidence_statement: Complete post-mortem examination documenting wound paths, bullet recovery, and firing distance
properties.context_description: This is the foundational forensic document of the case — its findings have never been disputed

instances.label: LAPD investigation files
instances.description: Los Angeles Police Department case files including witness interviews, evidence logs, and the Special Unit Senator (SUS) investigation records

properties.evidence_statement: LAPD conducted the primary investigation, filing over 50,000 pages of documents
properties.context_description: Many files were sealed for 20 years; some evidence (door frames, ceiling panels) was destroyed

instances.label: Trial transcripts — People v. Sirhan (1969)
instances.description: Complete trial record of People of the State of California v. Sirhan Bishara Sirhan

properties.evidence_statement: Trial proceedings including all testimony, cross-examination, and legal arguments
properties.context_description: Sirhan's defense did not contest that he fired a gun — they pursued a diminished capacity defense

instances.label: Lessig ChatGPT experiment
instances.description: Lawrence Lessig's published conversation testing ChatGPT on 'Did Sirhan Sirhan kill RFK?' — the model called the contradiction merely 'apparent' and gave a non-committal answer

properties.evidence_statement: ChatGPT acknowledged the contradiction but called it merely 'apparent' rather than 'actual'
properties.context_description: Lessig wrote this convinced him to 'stop worrying about AI' — LLMs correlate words but don't reason with stubborn facts

instances.label: People v. Sirhan (1969)
instances.description: Criminal trial of Sirhan Sirhan for the first-degree murder of Robert F. Kennedy, held in Los Angeles Superior Court

instances.label: Parole hearings
instances.description: Multiple parole hearings for Sirhan Sirhan over decades, including the notable 2016 hearing where Paul Schrade testified

instances.label: Reinvestigation petitions
instances.description: Various legal petitions filed to reopen and reinvestigate the RFK assassination case based on forensic contradictions

instances.label: Official narrative: Sirhan acted alone
instances.description: The prosecution's position that Sirhan Sirhan was the sole shooter and that his conviction is sound

instances.label: Second gun hypothesis
instances.description: The argument that physical and forensic evidence is fundamentally inconsistent with Sirhan as the sole shooter — a second gun fired from behind RFK

instances.label: Evidence integrity argument
instances.description: The argument that the chain of custody and handling of physical evidence was compromised — LAPD destroyed door frames and ceiling panels

instances.label: Lessig's reasoning: you cannot be in front and kill from behind
instances.description: The simple logical argument from Lawrence Lessig: if you are in front of someone, you cannot shoot them from behind — this is an actual contradiction, not an apparent one

instances.label: Direction of fire contradiction
instances.description: ACTUAL CONTRADICTION: The autopsy conclusively determined all shots entered RFK from behind. All witnesses unanimously placed Sirhan in front of RFK. You cannot be in front of someone and shoot them from behind.

properties.evidence_statement: Autopsy: shots from behind. Witnesses: Sirhan was in front. These two facts cannot both be true if Sirhan was the only shooter.
properties.context_description: This is the central contradiction of the case — the one Lessig highlighted and ChatGPT dismissed as merely 'apparent'

instances.label: Muzzle distance contradiction
instances.description: ACTUAL CONTRADICTION: Autopsy shows muzzle distance of 1-3 inches (contact range). Witnesses say Sirhan was never closer than 1.5 to 2 feet (18-24 inches).

properties.evidence_statement: The gun that killed RFK was 1-3 inches away. Sirhan was 18-72 inches away. The gun and Sirhan were not in the same place.
properties.context_description: Powder burn evidence is among the most reliable forensic indicators — it cannot be faked or misinterpreted

instances.label: Bullet count contradiction
instances.description: POSSIBLE CONTRADICTION: Total bullet holes and fragments may exceed the 8-round capacity of Sirhan's revolver

properties.evidence_statement: 7 bullets in victims + at least 2 in door frames = 9+ bullets from an 8-round gun
properties.context_description: Definitive resolution is impossible because LAPD destroyed the door frames

instances.label: Ballistics evidence mismatch
instances.description: ACTUAL CONTRADICTION: Independent criminalist Harper found that bullets from RFK and from bystander Weisel show different rifling characteristics — inconsistent with a single weapon

properties.evidence_statement: Wolfer says one gun. Harper says two guns. Both based on the same physical bullets.
properties.context_description: Harper filed a formal affidavit; Wolfer's methodology was later criticized by multiple experts

instances.label: Sirhan was the sole shooter
instances.description: The official claim, upheld by the conviction, that Sirhan Sirhan alone fired all shots that struck RFK and bystanders

instances.label: Sirhan fired from the front
instances.description: Established fact supported by all eyewitnesses: Sirhan was positioned in front of and facing Robert F. Kennedy when he fired

instances.label: RFK was shot from behind
instances.description: Autopsy conclusion: all three bullets that struck RFK entered from behind and to the right, at upward angles

instances.label: A second gunman was present
instances.description: Hypothesis that another person fired from behind RFK — potentially Thane Cesar or an unknown individual

instances.label: More than 8 bullets were fired
instances.description: Claim that the total number of bullet impacts exceeds the 8-round capacity of Sirhan's revolver

instances.label: Fatal shots fired at contact range
instances.description: Autopsy finding: powder burns indicate the gun was 1 to 3 inches from RFK when fatal shots were fired

instances.label: Sirhan was 1.5 to 6 feet away
instances.description: Witness testimony: Sirhan was never closer than 1.5 feet from RFK according to the closest witness (Uecker), and further according to others

instances.label: Guilty — first-degree murder
instances.description: Sirhan Sirhan was found guilty of first-degree murder of Robert F. Kennedy on April 17, 1969

instances.label: Death sentence (commuted to life)
instances.description: Sirhan was sentenced to death, later commuted to life imprisonment when California abolished the death penalty in 1972

instances.label: Ambassador Hotel, Los Angeles
instances.description: Historic hotel where RFK gave his California primary victory speech and was assassinated in the kitchen pantry

instances.label: Ambassador Hotel kitchen pantry
instances.description: Narrow kitchen pantry corridor where the shooting occurred — RFK was taking a shortcut to the press room

instances.label: RFK shooting — June 5, 1968
instances.description: At approximately 12:15 AM on June 5, 1968, Robert F. Kennedy was shot in the kitchen pantry of the Ambassador Hotel moments after his California primary victory speech

instances.label: California primary victory speech
instances.description: RFK's victory speech in the Ambassador Hotel ballroom after winning the California Democratic presidential primary — delivered minutes before the shooting

instances.label: Assessment: autopsy vs. witness positions
instances.description: Structured fact-check: the autopsy findings CANNOT be reconciled with Sirhan's known position. This is not a matter of interpretation — it is a physical impossibility.

properties.evidence_statement: If Sirhan was in front of RFK (all witnesses agree), and RFK was shot from behind (autopsy proves), then Sirhan could not have fired the fatal shots
properties.context_description: This assessment follows a Case Record Fact Check workflow: gather evidence, assess support/conflicts, format conclusion

instances.label: Assessment: structured facts vs. LLM correlation
instances.description: When asked 'Did Sirhan kill RFK?', a standard LLM gives a wishy-washy answer because it correlates words rather than reasoning with stubborn facts. With structured evidence objects and reasoning workflows, the contradiction is unavoidable.

properties.evidence_statement: ChatGPT called the contradiction 'apparent'. With structured facts, it is clearly 'actual'. Words matter in law.
properties.context_description: This is the thesis of the article: LLMs correlate words with words in a promethean manner, but correlation is not always what you need — you need stubborn facts

instances.label: Assessment: Cesar's position matches forensic evidence
instances.description: Thane Eugene Cesar was positioned directly behind RFK, held a .22 caliber gun, and admitted drawing it — his position is consistent with the autopsy's direction-of-fire finding

properties.evidence_statement: Cesar was behind RFK (matching shot direction), at contact distance (matching powder burns), carrying a .22 (matching bullet caliber)
properties.context_description: This does not prove Cesar fired, but his position satisfies the forensic constraints that Sirhan's position does not

attributes.evidence_statement: Autopsy by Noguchi concluded all three bullets entered RFK from behind at close range
attributes.context_description: Official autopsy performed June 6, 1968 at LA County Coroner's office

attributes.evidence_statement: Powder burns indicate 1-3 inch muzzle distance
attributes.context_description: Powder tattooing is a reliable forensic indicator of firing distance

attributes.evidence_statement: Fatal bullet entered behind right ear, traveling forward
attributes.context_description: Wound path is physically impossible from a frontal shooter position

attributes.evidence_statement: All eyewitnesses placed Sirhan in front of Kennedy
attributes.context_description: No witness placed Sirhan behind Kennedy — the consensus is unanimous

attributes.evidence_statement: You cannot be in front of someone and shoot them from behind
attributes.context_description: This is an ACTUAL contradiction — not apparent, not complex, but logically irreconcilable

attributes.evidence_statement: 1-3 inches vs 18-72 inches — the gun and Sirhan were not in the same place
attributes.context_description: Powder burns cannot be faked; witness testimony on distance was consistent
